description: Tax rules for tax year 2025 — F-1 visa students at UIC

rules.description: F-1 student within first 5 calendar years — exempt from Substantial Presence Test

result.explanation: As an F-1 student within your first 5 calendar years in the U.S., you are automatically considered an 'Exempt Individual' and do not count your days toward the Substantial Presence Test. This means you are a Nonresident Alien for tax purposes.

rules.description: F-1 student beyond 5 calendar years — must apply Substantial Presence Test

result.explanation: You have been in the U.S. for more than 5 calendar years on an F-1 visa and meet the Substantial Presence Test (183 weighted days). You are now considered a Resident Alien for tax purposes and must file Form 1040 instead of 1040-NR.

rules.description: F-1 student beyond 5 calendar years — does NOT meet Substantial Presence Test

result.explanation: Even though you have been in the U.S. for more than 5 calendar years on an F-1 visa, you do not meet the Substantial Presence Test. You remain a Nonresident Alien for tax purposes.

rules.description: Form 8843 required for all F-1 students regardless of income

result.explanation: All F-1 students who spent any time in the U.S. during the tax year must file Form 8843, even if they had no income. This form declares your exempt individual status.

rules.description: FICA (Social Security + Medicare) tax exemption for F-1 students in first 5 years

result.explanation: F-1 students in their first 5 calendar years are exempt from FICA taxes (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%). If your employer withheld these taxes, you may be entitled to a refund. Check your W-2 boxes 4 and 6.

rules.description: Illinois state income tax applies to all income earned in Illinois

result.explanation: Illinois has a flat 4.95% income tax rate on all income earned in Illinois. As a student at UIC in Chicago, you likely need to file Illinois Form IL-1040-NR (Nonresident and Part-Year Resident Return).

treatySummary.description: Tax treaties may reduce or eliminate U.S. tax on certain types of income. Check the treaty module for your country.
